Partial Settlement Flow

Partial settlement flow

Overpayment process — 13 steps  ·  Click any image to expand

Intercom
Lenvi
Agents Portal / Loan OS
1
Request from customer
Customer requests an overpayment via Intercom
click to expand
The customer gets in touch via Intercom to request an overpayment. They will usually specify the amount and whether they want it applied to reduce the term (pay off sooner) or reduce the monthly amount. Confirm this with them before proceeding.
2
Create Intercom ticket
Create a tracker ticket in the settlements inbox
click to expand
Once the customer has confirmed they want to make a partial payment, create a tracker ticket in the settlements inbox. Fill in the ticket fields: agreement number, full or partial, settlement amount, and what the payment is applied to (term or amount). Link the conversation to the ticket and update the ticket notes with key details — when the quote was requested, the amount, and any relevant context.
3
Navigate to Lenvi agreement
Find the customer's agreement on Lenvi
click to expand
Find the customer's agreement on Lenvi using their agreement number (e.g. MC000000292). Open the agreement page where you can see the balance, arrears, and actions available.
4
Actions to Settlements
Click the Actions button and select Settlements
click to expand
On the agreement page, click the Actions button in the bottom left and select Settlements from the dropdown menu. This will take you to the settlements page where you can view existing quotes and create new ones.
5
Create a new settlement quote
Scroll to the bottom and click Create Settlement
click to expand
You will see two sections: Full Settlement Quotes and Partial Settlement Quotes. Since you are generating a brand new quote, scroll to the bottom of the page and click Create Settlement.
6
Fill in the partial settlement form
Select Partial, enter lump sum, choose Term or Amount, hit Calculate
click to expand
Select Partial as the settlement type. Fill in the Lump Sum field with the requested overpayment amount. Under Solve For, select either Term (to shorten the loan end date) or Amount (to reduce the monthly instalment) — confirm with the customer which they prefer. Click Calculate.
7
Review the new schedule
Check how the term or amount changes, then Save
click to expand
Once calculated, you can see the updated repayment schedule. For overpayments applied to the term, scroll to the end of both the current and new schedules to compare the end dates and see how much shorter the loan is. For overpayments applied to the amount, the term stays the same but the instalment decreases. When happy, click Save at the top.
8
View the saved partial settlement quote
The new quote appears under Partial Settlement Quotes
click to expand
After saving, you will see the new quote listed under Partial Settlement Quotes with the settlement amount, quote date, valid until date, and effective date. Now you need to generate the payment link for the customer.
9
Go to Agents Portal
Search by agreement number, navigate to Loan Agreements then Loan OS
click to expand
Open the Agents Portal and search for the customer using their agreement number. Navigate to the Products section and click on Loan Agreements. You will see buttons for Loan OS and Lenvi — click Loan OS.
10
Fill in the payment details
Enter amount, description, and due date then Create payment
click to expand
Fill in the form: set the Amount to the overpayment amount, enter the User Facing Description as the agreement number followed by Partial Payment (e.g. MC000000292 - Partial Payment), and set the Due Date to the effective date from the Lenvi quote. Click Create payment to generate a payment link.
11
Monitor payment on Lenvi
Check Lenvi to confirm payment received
click to expand
Keep an eye on Lenvi to confirm when the payment comes through — the arrears section will reflect the incoming payment.
12
Take up the payment on Lenvi
Actions then Settlements then click Take Up
click to expand
Once confirmed, click Actions then Settlements. You will see the partial settlement quote with a Take Up button — click it to apply the payment to the agreement. Then resolve and close the ticket.
13
Confirm with customer, update ticket and close
Email customer, update Intercom ticket notes, resolve and close
click to expand
In Intercom, search for the macro Car Finance - Settlement quote. Fill in the details — paste in the payment hyperlink, and edit the macro to reflect whether the payment applies to the term or the amount. Send it to the customer. Update the tracker ticket notes with when the quote was sent, the amount, and the expiry date. Then resolve and close the ticket.
click anywhere outside to close · press Esc to close
